text:
There once was a lonely old man named Henry. He lived all alone in a big old house on the coast.

One evening, Henry decided to go for a walk. He wandered through the beautiful seaside park finding flowers everywhere you'd imagine flowers wanting to be. When he heard laughter he followed, watching a young couple play on the sands.

The man was named Oliver, and he was Henry's favorite person. They met years ago when Oliver fell into the ocean on their first date. Henry rescued him, and ever since that day they'd spend hours sharing stories and cracking each other up.

Henry found a beautiful old book on birds nearby. He spent hours reading about every bird he could imagine.

One sunny day, as he wandered further down the beach than ever before, he noticed something moving along the dunes. It was a raven! But this particular raven was different.

It spoke with a silky voice. And it had a book tucked under its wing.

"Why don't you sit?"
